title: Audit Atelier full-window module vs workbench-pane mounting and specify CKC/PoseKit/Ingest full-size sub-module rewiring
audit_verdict: HYBRID confirmed. Atelier is module-reach but pane-render with note/terminal chrome (PaneHeader + outer pane TabBar) plus an inner CKC/PoseKit/Ingest tab strip = double tab bar. 2x2 grid is dormant (Notes/WP-KERNEL-012 scope). Legacy React dead. Disposition: P1/P2/P3 -> MT-006 remediation; P4 menu-bar -> MT-041; P5 settings -> MT-042. Findings: MT-040-atelier-window-audit.json.
operator_request: Handshake currently splits everything into sub-windows/panes with tabs inside the main window (VS Code style). The operator WANTS to keep that compartmentalized pane-in-main-window pattern, but ONLY under the Notes Module (WP-KERNEL-012), which is in parallel live development and is the origin worktree of this WP. Atelier is now a MODULE and should open a FULL windowed experience. The operator believes it is still wired as if it is a compartmentalized window inside the main window (like a note or terminal pane). CKC, INGEST, and PoseKit are sub-modules of Atelier and should be full-size tabbed panels (each full size). The operator thinks current work (MT-004/MT-006) did not fully address this and asked for an extra inspection MT to inspect code and determine what needs changing before implementation continues.

disposition_directive.directive: Operator expects MT-006 will need to be redone in parts. After the audit, LINK the remediation back to MT-006 (redo MT-006 in parts as MT-006 remediation). Only if a finding is TRULY out of MT-006 scope may a new MT be created.
disposition_directive.new_mt_rule: Create a new implementation MT (MT-041+) ONLY for a finding genuinely outside MT-006's scope; state the concrete rationale for why it exceeds MT-006 scope.

markdown_projection.reason: JSON contract is authority; no .md projection created by default per packet artifact_policy and operator no-default-md directive. Generate on explicit operator request only.

lifecycle.audit_relationship_note: Inspects the outputs of MT-004 (COMPLETED, renamed CKC shell to Atelier) and MT-006 (READY_FOR_VALIDATION, promoted Atelier to a filling PaneType::AtelierEditor with CKC/PoseKit/Ingest sibling tabs). This MT does NOT depend on those MTs being validator-confirmed; it inspects their current code state. MT-006 is the primary audit target because it implemented Atelier as PaneType::AtelierEditor (the workbench pane mechanism also used by notes/terminals), which is the operator's suspected root cause.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: If a no-context model resumes this MT: this is a READ_ONLY inspection MT. Do not edit product code. Inspect the native GUI shell wiring in ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul to determine whether Atelier is mounted as a top-level full-window MODULE (module_switcher.rs / ModuleId path) or as a workbench PANE (PaneType::AtelierEditor / pane_registry.rs / pane_header.rs path, the same mechanism notes and terminals use). Emit MT-040-atelier-window-audit.json. Verify git -C ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul status shows no product changes beyond inherited baseline.

scope.summary: Read-only audit of how the Atelier surface is mounted in the native Handshake GUI shell. Determine with file:line evidence whether Atelier opens as a top-level FULL-WINDOW MODULE or is still hosted inside the workbench PANE grid (compartmentalized sub-window like notes/terminals). Establish the correct target model, produce a concrete rewiring change plan, and recommend whether the fix lands as MT-006 remediation or as one/more new implementation MTs. No product code is edited by this MT.

target_model.atelier: Top-level, full-window MODULE experience. When Atelier is active it owns the full work area (module-level), not a pane inside the workbench grid.
target_model.atelier_sub_modules: CKC, INGEST, and PoseKit are full-size tabbed sub-modules INSIDE Atelier. Selecting a tab fills the Atelier module area.
target_model.notes_module_reservation: The compartmentalized VS-Code-style pattern (multiple sub-windows/panes with tabs inside the main window) is INTENDED and must be PRESERVED, but only under the Notes Module (WP-KERNEL-012). The audit change plan must not regress Notes/workbench pane behavior.

scope.inspect_notes: inspect_paths are the known candidate surfaces from packet.relevant_product_files_initial and MT-006.scope.target_files. The auditor MUST also follow references from these files to the real Notes/terminal module and pane mounting so the module-vs-pane contrast is grounded, and MUST confirm whether any legacy React/TSX Atelier path (App.tsx/AtelierPanel.tsx) is still live or already superseded by the native Rust surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Document the current Atelier mount path with file:line evidence. State exactly whether Atelier is reached via a top-level module (module_switcher.rs / ModuleId) and how it is then rendered: as a full-window module region, or as a workbench PaneType (PaneType::AtelierEditor via pane_registry.rs / pane_header.rs). Name the exact enums, types, and factory functions.

AC2: For contrast, document with file:line evidence how the Notes module and at least one genuine pane (terminal or note) are mounted, to establish which mechanism is a full-window module and which is a compartmentalized pane-in-main-window.

AC3: Confirm or refute the operator's observation with concrete evidence. When the Atelier top-level entry is activated, does Atelier fill the entire work area as a module, or does it open inside the pane/workbench grid still showing pane chrome (pane header, sibling panes, split handles)? Base the verdict on code paths plus, where practical, a read-only AccessKit/Argus snapshot; do not assert from assumption.

AC4: Determine whether CKC/PoseKit/Ingest are currently pane-internal tabs (inside atelier_panel.rs within a PaneType) or module-internal full-size tabs, and what must change for them to be full-size tabbed sub-modules of a full-window Atelier module.
AC5: Define the target model precisely (Atelier = full-window module; CKC/INGEST/PoseKit = full-size tabbed sub-modules; compartmentalized pane-in-main pattern reserved to Notes/WP-KERNEL-012).

AC6: Produce a concrete change plan naming exact files, types, and the minimal rewiring to move Atelier from pane-hosted to module-hosted (or, if it is already module-hosted, name the actual defect). Include how CKC/PoseKit/Ingest tabs relocate and which stable AccessKit author_ids (module-ckc, atelier-tab-ckc/posekit/ingest, content regions) are preserved vs changed.
AC7: State that the change plan does NOT alter or regress the Notes/WP-KERNEL-012 pane/workbench model (KERNEL-012 is in parallel live development).

AC8: Disposition per operator directive (see disposition_directive): the DEFAULT is to redo MT-006 in parts as MT-006 remediation and LINK the audit's change-plan parts back to MT-006. Break the change plan into parts; for each part, state whether it is IN MT-006 scope (-> MT-006 remediation, the default) or TRULY OUT of MT-006 scope (-> propose a new MT-041+ with concrete rationale for why it exceeds MT-006 scope). Name the HBR obligations the implementation work must carry: HBR-VIS (Argus inspect/steer of the changed surface), HBR-QUIET (no focus/keyboard/mouse steal), HBR-MAN (in-product UserManual update).
AC9: Emit findings as machine-readable MT-040-atelier-window-audit.json. Leave product code untouched.
proof_targets: MT-040-atelier-window-audit.json exists, parses as JSON, and contains: current-mount-path evidence (AC1), notes/pane contrast (AC2), operator-observation verdict with evidence (AC3), tab-model finding (AC4), target model (AC5), change plan (AC6), notes-non-regression statement (AC7), and disposition recommendation (AC8).
Every finding cites concrete file:line evidence in ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul.
git -C ../wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul status --short --branch shows no new product changes beyond the inherited baseline dirty files listed in packet.baseline.inherited_dirty_files.

gui_obligation.not_applicable_reason: READ_ONLY_INSPECTION MT. It changes no operator-visible surface; it only inspects existing wiring and emits a governance-side audit artifact. Read-only AccessKit/Argus snapshots MAY be used as inspection evidence but no GUI is created or steered destructively. GUI creation and Argus proof obligations transfer to the follow-on implementation MT(s) identified by AC8.

user_manual_obligation.not_applicable_reason: No Handshake product behavior, tool, surface, command, or contract is created or changed by this read-only audit MT, so there is nothing to document in the in-product UserManual. The follow-on implementation MT(s) MUST carry the HBR-MAN UserManual obligation for the Atelier module/sub-module change.

hbr_followup_note: This inspection MT carries no runtime/VIS/QUIET/MAN/SWARM evidence obligations (consistent with the WP's READ_ONLY inspection MTs MT-001/MT-003). The implementation MT(s) recommended by AC8 MUST carry HBR-VIS (Argus), HBR-QUIET (no focus/input steal), HBR-MAN (UserManual), and HBR-SWARM (parallel-agent addressability of the module and its sub-module tabs).

handoff.expected_output: MT-040-atelier-window-audit.json plus a disposition that DEFAULTS to MT-006 remediation-in-parts (link change-plan parts back to MT-006 per operator directive 2026-07-01), with a new MT proposed only for a finding truly out of MT-006 scope. This is a scoping/diagnosis MT; it produces analysis and a change plan, not product runtime behavior, so it does not claim the windowing is fixed.

risks.risk: Auditor conflates the workbench PANE mechanism with the top-level MODULE mechanism and misdiagnoses the root cause.
risks.failure_scenario: The change plan targets the wrong layer and a later implementation MT rewires the pane system instead of promoting Atelier to a module.
risks.mitigation: AC1 and AC2 force explicit, file:line documentation of both the module path and the pane path, and an explicit Notes/terminal contrast before any verdict.

risks.risk: Auditor recommends a rewrite that breaks the Notes/WP-KERNEL-012 pane/workbench behavior the operator wants to keep.
risks.failure_scenario: Promoting Atelier to a full-window module also removes or regresses the pane-in-main-window model Notes depends on, damaging a parallel live-development worktree's target behavior.
risks.mitigation: AC7 requires an explicit non-regression statement for the Notes/workbench model; the change plan must isolate Atelier's module hosting from the shared pane system.

risks.risk: Auditor treats MT-006's implementer-focused PASS as proof and refutes the operator without inspecting the live GUI.
risks.failure_scenario: The audit concludes 'already correct' from MT-006's focused AccessKit tests while the running GUI still shows compartmentalized pane behavior.
risks.mitigation: AC3 requires the verdict to rest on code paths plus, where practical, a read-only live AccessKit/Argus snapshot, not on MT-006's self-reported test status.

risks.risk: Auditor breaks or proposes breaking stable AccessKit author_ids that parallel agents rely on (module-ckc, atelier-tab-*).
risks.failure_scenario: Downstream agent steering and existing tests break because stable IDs were changed without accounting.
risks.mitigation: AC6 requires enumerating preserved vs changed stable IDs explicitly.

risks.risk: Auditor drifts into implementing the fix inside this read-only MT.
risks.failure_scenario: Product code is edited under a READ_ONLY inspection MT, contaminating the WP's per-MT change discipline.
risks.mitigation: product_edit_allowed is false; proof_targets require git status to show no product changes; implementation is explicitly deferred to the AC8 follow-on MT(s).

minimum_controls: No product-code edits under this MT; only the governance-side audit artifact is written.
Verdict must cite file:line evidence and, where practical, a read-only live AccessKit/Argus snapshot.
Change plan must be explicitly non-regressive to the Notes/WP-KERNEL-012 pane/workbench model.
Preserved vs changed stable AccessKit author_ids must be enumerated.
Disposition (reopen MT-006 vs new MT-041+) must be an explicit recommendation, not an implicit decision.
